texts.full_text: rélat: does it exist? The sign of Leser-Trélat is usally associated with the appearance or rapid growth and worsening of multiple seborrheic keratoses in response to an internal malignancy, typically a gastrointestinal adenocarcinoma. This phenomenon was first described by Alfred Velpeau (Leser) and Ulysse Trélat. The concept is controversial because while some case reports have documented this association convincingly enough for it be considered as part of the paraneoplastic syndromes—conditions that arise from cancer but are not directly related to tumor invasion—the evidence supporting a direct causal relationship between new or worsening seborrheic keratoses and internal malignancy is sparse. Many dermatologists argue about its validity, noting instances where these skin changes occur without any underlying neoplasm. In clinical practice today: 1. Clinical Vigilance: If multiple lesions appear suddenly in an adult who has never had them before or if known seborrheic keratoses rapidly increase significantly (Leser-Trélat sign), it might prompt a more thorough investigation for internal malignancies, especially gastrointestinal ones. 2. Diagnostic Approach: - A detailed patient history and physical examination. - Imaging studies such as CT scans of the chest or abdomen may be warranted to look for occult cancers if other symptoms suggest an underlying neoplasm (e.g., weight loss, abdominal pain). 3. Posted in Uncategorized | Leave a comment Great New Review for Johnny Graphic Posted on June 13, 2014 by drmar120 My middle-grade ghost adventure, Johnny Graphic and the Etheric Bomb, has been out a while now. But it’s still collecting great reviews. Just this week, Jessica Kosinski of A Book a Day Reviews posted her impressions of my 1930s pulp-style yarn, awarding it five stars! Here’s some of what she had to say: There are a few things that I like about this book. One of them is the idea of ghosts co-existing with, and even being able to interact with, human beings. Whether you believe in ghosts or not, you have to admit that the idea is intriguing. I also enjoyed the fact that the author went out of his way to give each ghost in the story his or her own unique personality… Another thing I enjoyed about the book is that it takes place in an alternate 1935. The locations all have different names than you might expect. There was a Civil War of sorts that ended differently from the Civil War we all know, causing a different division of countries, and a different governmental system. Granted, that also meant that I, as the reader, had to just accept certain things as fact and didn’t have a lot of familiar reference points, but I liked the imagination behind the concept. texts.full_text: Even when the sensor is accessible, it might still involve inconvenience or costs associated with battery replacement. To overcome these limitations in powering sensors for property monitoring applications—especially those that are difficult-to-reach by conventional means such as solar panels and electrical outlets—an innovative solution has been developed: wireless energy transfer. This technology enables the transmission of electric charge through space, eliminating any need to physically connect a sensor with wires or batteries. One popular form is inductive powering which uses magnetic fields for transferring electricity between two coils; one coil on your device (sensor) and another attached nearby source area acting as an energy transmitter. The benefits are significant—not only does it remove the hassle of frequent battery changes, but also reduces installation costs by eliminating or minimizing wiring. In addition to inductive powering systems there exist other wireless charging methods like resonant coupling where devices receive charge using electromagnetic fields that match their natural frequency resonance with a transmitting source. These types are ideal for situations requiring contact-free and flexible positioning of sensors without the needfor fixed installation positions or physical connections, making them highly suitable to monitor various property conditions. On one side stands Democrat Claire McCaskill's chosen successor and former state auditor Chris Koster — a moderate candidate known for his willingness to work across the aisle during years in office. On issues like Medicaid expansion, he has shown openness that contrasts sharply against national Democratic trends of strong advocacy; instead advocating more cautious approaches tailored specifically Missouri’s unique fiscal situation. On the Republican side is Eric Greitens—a political newcomer who built a reputation through humanitarian and military service before entering politics—whose stance on legislation represents an interesting deviation from traditional GOP orthodoxy. While he supports it, his rationale focuses less on party lines than what benefits workers in Missouri most effectively over time. Both candidates have articulated clear visions for the state that often diverge significantly not only with each other but also sometimes within their own parties’ broader agendas nationally or even statewide at times. this dynamic has created a campaign environment where voters are being asked to evaluate these men based primarily on individual merit and specific policy proposals rather than simply along party lines. It's an opportunity for Missouri residents, tired of the usual partisan bickering seen in many other races across America this election cycle. texts.full_text: /ssh/sftp/) サーバー上のファイルを監視、作成、送信、および受信するタスクを自動化するには、Azure Logic Apps と SFTP コネクタを使用して統合ワークフローを構築して自動化できます。 SFTP は、任意の信頼性の高いデータ ストリームを通して、ファイル アクセス、ファイル転送、およびファイル管理を提供するネットワーク プロトコルです。 次に、自動化できるタスクの例をいくつか示します。 * ファイルの追加または変更を監視します。 * ファイルの取得、作成、コピー、更新、一覧、および削除を行います。 * ファイルの内容とメタデータを取得します。 * アーカイブをフォルダーに抽出します。 SFTP サーバー上のイベントを監視し、出力を他のアクションで使用できるようにするトリガーを使用できます。 SFTP サーバーで各種のタスクを実行するアクションを使用できます。 ロジック アプリ内の他のアクションでも、SFTP アクションからの出力を使用するようにできます。 たとえば、SFTP サーバーからファイルを定期的に取得する場合は、Office 365 Outlook コネクタまたは Outlook.com コネクタを使用して、これらのファイルやその内容に関するメールのアラートを送信できます。 ロジック アプリを初めて使用する場合は、「[Azure Logic Apps とは](../logic-apps/logic-apps-overview.md)」を参照してください。 ## 制限 SFTP コネクタで処理されるのは "*50 MB 以下*" のファイルのみであり、[メッセージ チャンク](../logic-apps/logic-apps-handle-large-messages.md)はサポートされません。 それを超えるファイルについては、[SFTP-SSH コネクタ](../connectors/connectors-sftp-ssh.md)を使用してください。 SFTP コネクタと SFTP-SSH コネクタの違いについては、SFTP-SSH に関する記事の「[SFTP-SSH と SFTP を比較する](../connectors/connectors-sftp-ssh.md#comparison)」で確認してください。 ## 前提条件 * Azure サブスクリプション。 Azure サブスクリプションがない場合は、[無料の Azure アカウントにサインアップ](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/)してください。 * SFTP サーバーのアドレスとアカウントの資格情報。これにより、ロジック アプリが SFTP アカウントにアクセスできます。 [Secure Shell (SSH)](https://www.ssh.com/ssh/protocol/) プロトコルを使用するには、SSH 秘密キーと SSH 秘密キーのパスワードへのアクセスも必要です。 > [!NOTE] > > SFTP コネクタは、次の秘密キーの形式をサポートしています: OpenSSH、ssh.com、および PuTTY。 > > ロジック アプリを作成している場合は、必要な SFTP トリガーまたはアクションを追加した後、SFTP サーバーの接続情報を指定する必要があります。 > SSH 秘密キーを使おうとしている場合は、SSH 秘密キー ファイルからキーを***コピー***し、そのキーを接続の詳細に***貼り付ける***ようにしてください。***キーは手動で入力または編集しないでください***。そのようにすると、接続が失敗することがあります。 > 詳細については、この記事で後述する手順を参照してください。 * [ロジック アプリの作成方法](../logic-apps/quickstart-create-first-logic-app-workflow.md)に関する基本的な知識 * SFTP アカウントにアクセスするロジック アプリ。 SFTP トリガーで開始するには、[空のロジック アプリを作成](../logic-apps/quickstart-create-first-logic-app-workflow.md)します。 SFTP アクションを使用するには、**繰り返し**トリガーなど、別のトリガーでロジック アプリを開始します。 ## SFTP トリガーの動作 SFTP トリガーは、SFTP ファイル システムをポーリングし、前回のポーリング以降に変更されたファイルを探すことによって動作します。 一部のツールでは、ファイルが変更されたときにタイムスタンプを保持できます。 これらの場合は、トリガーが動作できるように、この機能を無効にする必要があります。 一般的な設定をいくつか次に示します。 | SFTP クライアント | アクション | |-------------|--------| | Winscp | **[オプション]** > **[ユーザー設定]** > **[転送]** > **[編集]** > **[Preserve timestamp] (タイムスタンプを保持する)** > **[無効]** に移動する | | FileZilla | **[転送]** > **[Preserve timestamps of transferred files] (転送されたファイルのタイムスタンプを保持する)** > **[無効]** に移動する | ||| トリガーによって新しいファイルが検出されると、トリガーはその新しいファイルが完了していて、すべて書き込まれていることを確認します。 たとえば、トリガーがファイル サーバーを確認している際に、ファイルの進行状態が変化する場合があります。 部分的に書き込まれたファイルが返されることなないように、トリガーは最新の変更を含むファイルのタイムスタンプをメモしますが、すぐにそのファイルを返すことはありません。 トリガーは、もう一度サーバーをポーリングしてファイルを返します。 場合によっては、この動作によって、最大でトリガーのポーリング間隔が 2 倍となる遅延が生じる場合があります。 ## SFTP への接続 [!INCLUDE [Create connection general intro](../../includes/connectors-create-connection-general-intro.md)] 1. [Azure portal](https://portal.azure.com) にサインインし、ロジック アプリ デザイナーでロジック アプリを開きます (まだ開いていない場合)。 1. 空のロジック アプリの場合: 検索ボックスに、フィルターとして「SFTP」と入力します。 トリガーの一覧で、目的のトリガーを選択します。 または 既存のロジック アプリの場合、アクションを追加する最後のステップの下で、 **[新しいステップ]** を選択します。 検索ボックスに、フィルターとして「sftp」と入力します。 アクションの一覧で、目的のアクションを選択します。 ステップの間にアクションを追加するには、ステップ間の矢印の上にポインターを移動します。 表示されるプラス記号 ( **+** ) を選択し、 **[アクションの追加]** を選択します。 1. 接続に必要な詳細を指定します。 > [!IMPORTANT] > > **SSH 秘密キー** プロパティに SSH 秘密キーを入力する場合は、次の追加の手順に従ってください。これは、このプロパティの完全で正しい値を確実に指定する助けになります。 > 無効なキーを指定すると、接続が失敗します。 任意のテキスト エディターを使用できますが、次に例として、Notepad.exe を使用してキーを正しくコピーして貼り付ける方法を示す手順の例を示します。 1. テキスト エディターで SSH 秘密キーのファイルを開きます。 これらの手順では、例としてメモ帳を使用します。 1. メモ帳の **[編集]** メニューで、 **[すべて選択]** を選択します。 1. **[編集]** > **[コピー]** を選択します。 1. 追加した SFTP トリガーまたはアクションで、コピーした*完全な*キーを **SSH 秘密キー** プロパティ (これは、複数行をサポートします) に貼り付けます。 キーは***貼り付けるようにしてください***。 ***キーを手動で入力または編集しないでください***。 1. 接続の詳細の入力を完了したら、 **[作成]** を選択します。 1. 選択したトリガーまたはアクションのために必要な詳細を指定し、ロジック アプリのワークフローの構築を続けます。 ## 例 ### SFTP トリガー:When a file is added or modified (ファイルの追加または変更時) このトリガーは、SFTP サーバーでファイルが追加または変更されたときにロジック アプリ ワークフローを開始します。 たとえば、ファイルの内容をチェックし、内容が指定された条件を満たすかどうかに基づいてその内容を取得する条件を追加できます。 その後、ファイルの内容を取得し、その内容を SFTP サーバーのフォルダーに格納するアクションを追加できます。 **企業での使用例**: このトリガーを使用して、SFTP フォルダーに顧客からの注文を表す新しいファイルがあるかどうかを監視できます。 その後、さらに処理するために注文の内容を取得し、その注文を注文データベース内に格納できるように、**ファイルの内容を取得する**などの SFTP アクションを使用できます。 ### SFTP アクション:コンテンツを取得する このアクションは、SFTP サーバー上のファイルの内容を取得します。 そのため、たとえば前の例のトリガーと、ファイルの内容が満たす必要がある条件を追加できます。 条件が

Safety Equipment Check-Up: - Inspect tires on both the towing vehicle and travel trailer for adequate pressure. - Ensure that safety chains are properly attached; they should cross under your hitch to prevent dragging if it detaches from tow ball unexpectedly (the "rule of four"). 5. Proper Hitch Height: Adjusting or adding a shank can ensure proper weight distribution, which is critical in maintaining control and stability while driving. 6. Brake Controller Installation: - If your trailer has electric brakes that are not functioning with the brake pedal (commonly referred to as "dead braking"), consider installing an automatic electronic controller for better safety. 7. Waterproofing Essentials: Make sure all vents, windows and door seals in both vehicles/trailers are properly sealed against leaks during rainstorms. 8. Utility Management: - Familiarize yourself with connecting water lines to prevent any spills or mishaps; consider using flexible hoses for easier maneuverability. 9. Power Solutions: - Invest time understanding your power setup, whether it's a generator (and its safe operation), solar panels if applicable and battery management systems. 10. Storage Organization: Plan how you will store items in the trailer to maximize space without overloading any one area; distribute weight evenly for better handling on roads. 11. Emergency Kit: - Keep an emergency kit with tire repair tools, a flashlight (or headlamp), extra fuses/bulbs/extension cords and basic first aid supplies. 12. texts.full_text: ing on forms and models within ruby's actionView framework (Rails), there are times where we might want a field not be validated based upon some condition such as role etc., this is the concept of skipping validations. In your case with `inclusion` validator you can bypass it using Rails' built-in methods like: ``` object.validate_field_name, false ``` The second argument being 'false', will skip that particular validation. Another way to do so would be by overriding validate method in the model: ```ruby def valid? validate_exclusions.each { send("#{field}="), nil } super(only: end ``` In this example, `exclude_field` is an array of attributes you want to skip. However it's important that we should use these methods cautiously as bypassing validations might lead your data into inconsistent or invalid state. If the question was something else related with Rails validator please provide more details for a precise answer. I hope this helps!

And I failed to navigate to a new View after clicking YES button. <fx:Script> <![CDATA[ protected function some_handler():void { (new AlertMsg()).open(this,false); } ]]> </fx:Script> <fx:Declarations> <fx:Component className="AlertMsg"> <s:SkinnablePopUpContainer x="70" y="300"> <s:TitleWindow title="MSG" close="close()"> <s:VGroup horizontalAlign="center" paddingTop="8" paddingBottom="8" paddingLeft="8" paddingRight="8" gap="5" width="100%"> <s:Label text="Some Alert MSG"/> <s:HGroup> <s:Button label="YES" click="{close(); navigateToView(new YesView());"/> <s:Button label="NO" click="{close();"/> </s:HGroup> </s:VGroup> </s:TitleWindow> </s:SkinnablePopUpContainer> </fx:Component> </fx:Declarations> In the code above, you can see that you're using `navigateToView(new YesView());` to navigate to a new view, `YesView`, when the "YES" button is clicked. Make sure that `YesView` is a properly defined view in your application. However, you're also calling `close();` before the navigation, which will close the `AlertMsg` component immediately after the button is clicked, potentially causing the navigation to be ignored if the close operation is faster. To fix this, you can create a function in your `AlertMsg` component to handle the button click, close the `AlertMsg` first, and then navigate to the new view. Here is a revised version of your code: ```xml <fx:Script> <![CDATA[ public function navigateToYesView():void { close(); navigateToView(new YesView()); } ]]> </fx:Script> <fx:Declarations> <!-- ... your existing code ... --> <fx:Component className="AlertMsg"> <!-- ... your existing code ... --> <s:Button label="YES" click="{navigateToYesView();}"/> <!-- ... your existing code ... --> </s:TitleWindow> </s:SkinnablePopUpContainer> </fx:Component> </fx:Declarations> ``` Now, when the "YES" button is clicked, it will call the `navigateToYesView()` function, which

texts.full_text: Since the touch screen panel may replace an additional input device coupled to the image display device to operate such as a keyboard and a mouse, the use range is gradually increasing. Methods of realizing a touch screen panel include a resistance layer method, a photo-sensing method, and an electro-capacitive method. Among the above methods, the widely used electro-capacitive touch screen panel detects a change in capacitance formed by conductive sensing electrodes together with other peripheral sensing electrodes or a ground electrode when the human hands or the object contacts the touch screen panel to convert the contact position into the electrical signal. Here, in order to correctly determine the contact position in a contact surface, the sensing electrodes include first sensing electrodes formed to be coupled in a first direction by first connectors and second sensing electrodes formed to be coupled in a second direction by second connectors. In general, the first connectors and the second connectors intersect each other while being insulated from each other with an insulating layer interposed. The width of the first connectors and the second connectors is smaller than the width of the sensing electrodes so that resistance is relatively large. Therefore, intersections between the first connectors and the second connectors may be damaged by static electricity. texts.full_text: (FX) in India, Deborah (DS) in the USA and Ana in the UK who helped in the early phase. You worked beside the three "Jamgineers" Natasche, Markus and Adam both before and during the Jam. You helped to plan and execute the event and choose the fantastic Secret Theme. Thanks, guys, and to everyone else who helped - especially the local hosts, our film angel Ritika and her unsuspecting husband Ashish! We're sorry for the things that went wrong (blame your Jamgineers!), and we say THANKS for what went right! If you’re back at work already, then take a moment to look at a few projects, leave some feedback, share a few pictures on Facebook, tell a friend what you experienced and how it felt. Join us in saying thanks to the wonderful Hosts, supporters & EVERY Jammer too! The Global Service 2104 was amazing! And if you want to do it again - in a whole new context - then see you at Global GovJam in June! Jam hosts and Jammers in Tehran are already on their way to the Jam, ready to kick off the Global Service Jam 2015 in style! They'll be joined by Cairo a few hours later, then other middle eastern Jams on their Friday morning, at much the same time as the Jams in New Zealand and Australia also get in gear. texts.full_text: SpongeBob, Patrick, and Sandy as they go jellyfishing. Plot The riDe begins when the French narrator (Tom Kenny) tells the audience to put away their cameras, cell phones, and other electronics. The audience then "dives" underwater in a submarine, "hitting" an anchor in the process. It then lands on Jellyfish Fields, where SpongeBob (Kenny) and Patrick (Bill Fagerbakke) sing "The Jellyfish Song" (also heard on the episode "SpongeBob's Last Stand") while catching jellyfish. Then, Sandy (Carolyn Lawrence) shows up wiht a new invention, the Jellyfishing Machine, which catches an entire swarm of jellyfish at once. SpongeBob and Patrick are impressed with her machine, but then it malfunctions and releases all the jellyfish into the air. SpongeBob, Patrick, and Sandy chase after them in their submarine. They pass by a group of touRists (including Squidward, Mr. Krabs, Plankton, Larry the Lobster, Perch Perkins, and Mermaid Man) who are watching from the shore. The jellyfish then fly into the air and form a giant cloud that covers Bikini Bottom. The submarine crashes through Jellyfish Fields again, where SpongeBob, Patrick, and Sandy get caught in a net of jellyfish (which they think is a "jelly-mermaid"). They are saved by Plankton's flying machine. The trio then flies to the surface with Plankton, where they find that the jellyfish cloud has covered Bikini Bottom. This is particularly important in immunosuppressed hosts, where the immune response is compromised, and the host is more susceptible to viral infections. Clinical trials and case studies have shown that the use of BRMs in immunosuppressed hosts, such as cancer patients receiving chemotherapy, transplant patients on immunosuppressive therapy, and patients with inherited immunodeficiencies, can result in the reduction of viral infections and the amelioration of symptoms. For instance, interferons, a group of cytokines with antiviral properties, have been successfully used in the treatment of chronic viral infections, such as hepatitis B and C, as well as in preventing the recurrence of viral infections in immunosuppressed patients. texts.full_text: akening response, 4 morning cortisol samples were collected. Sleep and mindfulness were assessed by self-rating questionnaires. Among participants with long-term meditation experience, morning cortisol decreased with length of experience. For novices, after an 8-week introductory MBSR course, morning cortisol levels had decreased, while both sleep and self-attribution of mindfulness significantly improved. Cortisol levels did not, however, change between the beginning and end of individual MBSR sessions. The pattern of results lends support to the view that MBSr/meditation has a favorable influence both on biomarkers of stress regulation, such as cortisol secretion, and on sleep.

While typically benign, they can exhibit malignant behavior, withthe ability to invade surrounding brain tissue and spread to distant sites.Malignantchoroid plexus papillomas areeven more uncommon,with only a handful of cases reported in theliterature. The presentation of extraneuralmetastasis, as seen inthis case,isparticularly rare and signifies a poor prognosis. The patient,an 11-month-old child, presented withsigns ofincreasedintracranial pressure, including vomiting,irritability, anda bulging fontanelle. Imaging studies revealeda large, enhancing mass in thelateral ventricle, consistent with a choroid plexus papilloma. Despite the tumor's location and size, the child underwent a successful surgical resection. However, pathologicalexamination of the resected tissue revealed a malignantchoroidplexus papilloma. Followingsurgery, the child received chemotherapyand radiation therapy inan attempt to eradicate any residual tumor cells. Initially,therewasa partialresponse totreatment, with areduction in the size of thetumor. Unfortunately, the child's condition deterioratedover time, and imagingstudiesrevealed progression of the disease. The tumorrecurred and exhibited aggressive behavior, withthe developmentof extraneural metastasis to the scalp. This case report underscores the challenges in managing malignant choroidplexus papillomas, particularly inyoung children. It highlights the irrefutable attack on recent EU accession countries and the environment, as well as the cost this system has already had on EU taxpayers and European democracy. The ‘investor-state dispute settlement’ mechanism has been coming under growing publicscrutiny due to its potential impact on policy decisions and public budgets. This report aims to shed light on several key aspects of these cases, including: 1. Frequency and Distribution: The number of investor-state disputes initiated against the eight EU member states mentioned above since 1994 will be analyzed, with a particular focus on any trends or patterns that emerge in specific sectors or countries. 2. Environmental Impact: Special attention is given to cases where environmental regulations have been challenged by investors under international trade agreements and investment treaties. The report examines how these disputes affect the ability of governments to implement policies aimed at protecting natural resources, combating climate change, and ensuring public health standards are met without facing significant financial penalties or legal challenges from foreign corporations 3. Financial Costs: An assessment is made on both direct costs (legal fees) as well indirect economic impacts such potential loss in revenue due to policy changes resulting directly because of these disputes which could have implications for national budget allocations towards public services like healthcare, education etc., thereby impacting the quality life citizens enjoy 4. Democratic Implications: The report discuss further how this mechanism can undermine democratic processes within EU member states by limiting their regulatory sovereignty and policy space through threat of costly litigation brought forth under opaque arbitration procedures that lack transparency or accountability mechanisms typically found in domestic court systems 5. Palate: A classic Eden Valley Riesling with a tight structure, delicate fruit at this early stage of its life, with promise of deeper honeyed citrus notes to come after some time in the bottle. It is a zesty clean wine showing the wonderful crisp mineral acidity typical of top Eden Valley Riesling. Peter LehMann Winery The history of Peter Lehmann Wines is intrinsically linked to the events that made the Barossa famous. Formed in 1979 by Peter Lehmann to assist the grape growers of the region who at the time were facing financial ruin, they nowproduce some of Australia's most iconic wines from their cellar door and winery located on Para Road near Tanunda in South Australia. The estate offers a diverse range of high-quality red and white varietals including Riesling which is celebrated for its crisp acidity, delicate fruit flavors as well as the potential to develop richer honeyed notes with age - perfectly exemplified by this Eden Valley offering from Peter Lehmann Wines. texts.full_text: f the angiotensin converting enzyme (ACE) inhibitors captopril and enalapril and the calcium antagonist verapamil was studied in an experimental model using rat heart allografts. Daily post-tratransplantation, rats were treated with each drug or vehicle alone as control for 14 hours; the group gave cyclosporine A (CsA) from day -1 until the end of the experiment (positive control). Blood transplant survival was greatly prolonged in animals receiving captopril, enalapIterations, verapamil and CsA. survival period was 10+/-3 weeks for controls vs. >45-78 hours with medications. Histological investigation showed moderate rejection in untreated allografts but significant cellular penetration of cardiac neurons in drug-treated rats. Immunohistochemistry showed high intragraft concentrations of interferon (IFN)-gamma, interleukin (OH)-2 and IL-6 mRNAs in dogs treated with the drugs. All grafts expressed low concentrations of transforming growth factor (TGF) beta1 protein. However, therapy with an ACE inhibitor or a calcium antagonist prolonged heart transplant mortality in rats;

How does this grammar work and how does the nuance differ from 私が世界で一番偉いか？？ A: There is nothing incorrect or ungrammatical about the sentence: 「[私]{わたし}が[世界]{せかい}で[一番偉]{いちばんえら}いだって？？」 because this is different from saying: 「私が世界で一番偉いだ。」, which is ungrammatical. 「だって」 in the sentence in question is placed after a quote, is it not? 私が世界で一番偉いだって？？ ＝ 『私が世界で一番偉い。』だって？？ Depending on the context, it may be: 私が『世界で一番偉い』だって？？ This 「だって」 expresses one's surprise, criticism, etc. toward the statement that is being quoted. The fact that the quoted statement happens to end in an i-adjective has no effect on the validity of 「だって」. Since that is a valid usage, you can find it in dictionaries, too.
